title_en: The Mu'tazili Framework: Theological Positions and Their Influence
content_en:
To understand Al-Kashaf responsibly, one must understand the Mu'tazili theological framework that shapes it. The Mu'tazilah held a distinctive cluster of theological positions that set them apart from the Sunni mainstream — positions on divine attributes, divine justice, human free will, and the status of the Quran that al-Zamakhshari wove into his exegesis at crucial points.

The most consequential of these positions for Quranic interpretation is the Mu'tazili doctrine that the Quran is created (makhluq). This doctrine, which provoked the famous Mihna (Inquisition) under the Abbasid caliph al-Ma'mun in the third Islamic century — during which Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal was imprisoned for refusing to affirm it — stands in direct opposition to the Sunni consensus that the Quran is the uncreated speech of God. Al-Zamakhshari's commentary on verses touching on the divine word and the nature of revelation reflects this Mu'tazili position, sometimes explicitly and sometimes through his choice of interpretive alternatives.

The second major Mu'tazili influence is the doctrine of divine justice (al-adl), which the Mu'tazilah interpreted in a specific rationalist direction: God cannot do what is contrary to justice as reason perceives it. This led them to deny predestination in the Sunni sense and to emphasize human free will and moral accountability in a way that shapes how al-Zamakhshari reads verses about divine guidance, misguidance, and the sealing of hearts. Where Sunni exegetes read divine agency, al-Zamakhshari often reads human choice — interpreting God's 'sealing' of hearts as a description of what humans have done to themselves rather than a direct divine act.

The third influence is the Mu'tazili position on divine attributes (al-sifat): they denied the independent reality of the divine attributes (knowledge, power, will, etc.) and interpreted them as expressions of the divine essence, to avoid what they saw as a form of multiplicity in the divine being. This shapes how al-Zamakhshari handles the asma al-husna (divine names) and the attribute verses in the Quran.

Sunni scholars — Ash'ari, Maturidi, and Athari alike — identified and catalogued these theological interventions. Ibn al-Munir al-Iskandari (d. 683 AH), a Maliki scholar, wrote Al-Intisaf min Al-Kashaf (The Vindication Against Al-Kashaf), a comprehensive response to al-Zamakhshari's Mu'tazili glosses, refuting them from Sunni positions while quoting the Kashaf text verbatim. This work is often printed alongside Al-Kashaf in traditional editions, giving the reader both al-Zamakhshari's positions and the Sunni scholarly response on the same page.

Later critical readers include al-Taftazani, who addressed Al-Kashaf's theological positions in his commentary on theology, and numerous scholars in the Ottoman and Mughal scholarly traditions who incorporated Al-Kashaf into their tafsir curricula while requiring students to study its theological passages alongside the Sunni critiques. This parallel reading model — study the text, study the critique — became the standard method for engaging with Al-Kashaf in traditional Islamic education, and remains the appropriate model today.
title_id: Kerangka Mu'tazili: Posisi-Posisi Teologis dan Pengaruhnya
content_id:
Untuk memahami Al-Kashaf secara bertanggung jawab, seseorang harus memahami kerangka teologis Mu'tazilah yang membentuknya. Kaum Mu'tazilah memegang sekelompok posisi teologis yang khas yang membedakan mereka dari arus utama Sunni — posisi tentang sifat-sifat Ilahi, keadilan Ilahi, kehendak bebas manusia, dan status Al-Quran yang ditenun al-Zamakhshari ke dalam tafsirnya pada titik-titik krusial.

Yang paling berpengaruh dari posisi-posisi ini terhadap interpretasi Qurani adalah doktrin Mu'tazilah bahwa Al-Quran adalah makhluk (makhluq). Doktrin ini, yang memicu Mihna (Inquisisi) yang terkenal di bawah khalifah Abbasiyah al-Ma'mun pada abad ketiga Islam — di mana Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal dipenjara karena menolak mengiyakannya — bertentangan langsung dengan konsensus Sunni bahwa Al-Quran adalah kalam Allah yang tidak diciptakan. Komentar al-Zamakhshari pada ayat-ayat yang menyentuh firman Ilahi dan hakikat wahyu mencerminkan posisi Mu'tazilah ini, kadang secara eksplisit dan kadang melalui pilihannya terhadap alternatif-alternatif interpretatif.

Pengaruh Mu'tazilah kedua yang utama adalah doktrin keadilan Ilahi (al-adl), yang ditafsirkan oleh Mu'tazilah dalam arah rasionalis yang spesifik: Allah tidak dapat melakukan apa yang bertentangan dengan keadilan sebagaimana yang dipahami oleh akal. Hal ini mendorong mereka untuk menolak predestinasi dalam pengertian Sunni dan menekankan kehendak bebas manusia serta pertanggungjawaban moral dengan cara yang membentuk bagaimana al-Zamakhshari membaca ayat-ayat tentang hidayah Ilahi, penyesatan, dan penguncian hati. Di mana mufasir Sunni membaca agen Ilahi, al-Zamakhshari sering membaca pilihan manusia — menafsirkan 'penguncian' Allah atas hati sebagai deskripsi tentang apa yang telah dilakukan manusia terhadap diri mereka sendiri, bukan sebagai tindakan Ilahi langsung.

Pengaruh ketiga adalah posisi Mu'tazilah tentang sifat-sifat Ilahi (al-sifat): mereka menolak realitas independen sifat-sifat Ilahi (ilmu, kuasa, kehendak, dan sebagainya) dan menafsirkannya sebagai ekspresi dari zat Ilahi, untuk menghindari apa yang mereka anggap sebagai bentuk kemajemukan dalam dzat Ilahi. Hal ini membentuk cara al-Zamakhshari menangani asma al-husna (nama-nama Ilahi yang indah) dan ayat-ayat sifat dalam Al-Quran.

Para ulama Sunni — baik Asy'ari, Maturidi, maupun Athari — mengidentifikasi dan mengkatalogkan intervensi-intervensi teologis ini. Ibn al-Munir al-Iskandari (w. 683 H), seorang ulama Maliki, menulis Al-Intisaf min Al-Kashaf (Pembelaan dari Al-Kashaf), sebuah bantahan komprehensif terhadap komentar-komentar Mu'tazili al-Zamakhshari, mematahkannya dari posisi-posisi Sunni sambil mengutip teks Al-Kashaf secara verbatim. Karya ini sering dicetak bersama Al-Kashaf dalam edisi-edisi tradisional, memberi pembaca sekaligus posisi al-Zamakhshari dan respons keilmuan Sunni pada halaman yang sama.

Para pembaca kritis belakangan mencakup al-Taftazani, yang membahas posisi-posisi teologis Al-Kashaf dalam komentarnya tentang teologi, serta berbagai ulama dalam tradisi keilmuan Ottoman dan Mughal yang memasukkan Al-Kashaf ke dalam kurikulum tafsir mereka seraya mensyaratkan para pelajar untuk mempelajari ayat-ayat teologisnya bersama kritik-kritik Sunni. Model pembacaan paralel ini — belajar teksnya, belajar kritiknya — menjadi metode standar untuk mengkaji Al-Kashaf dalam pendidikan Islam tradisional, dan tetap menjadi model yang tepat hingga hari ini.
content_ar: لفهم Al-Kashaf responsibly, على المرء understand the Mu'tazili theological framework that shapes it. The المعتزلة held a distinctive cluster of theological positions that set them apart from the Sunni mainstream — positions on divine attributes, divine العدل, human free will, and the status of the القرآن الكريم that al-Zamakhshari wove into his exegesis at البالغ الأهمية points. The most consequential of these positions for القرآني interpretation is the Mu'tazili doctrine that the القرآن الكريم is created (makhluq). This doctrine, which provoked the famous Mihna (Inquisition) under the Abbasid caliph al-Ma'mun in the ثالثاً Islamic century — خلال which الإمام أحمد ابن حنبل was imprisoned for refusing to affirm it — stands in direct opposition to the Sunni consensus that the القرآن الكريم is the uncreated speech of God. Al-Zamakhshari's commentary on الآيات touching on the divine word and the nature of الوحي reflects this Mu'tazili position, sometimes explicitly and sometimes through his choice of interpretive alternatives. The ثانياً major Mu'tazili influence is the doctrine of divine العدل (al-adl), which the المعتزلة interpreted in a specific rationalist direction: God cannot do what is contrary to العدل as العقل perceives it. This led them to deny القضاء والقدر in the Sunni sense and to emphasize human free will and moral accountability in a الطريق that shapes how al-Zamakhshari reads الآيات about divine الهداية, misguidance, and the sealing of القلوب. Where Sunni exegetes read divine agency, al-Zamakhshari often reads human choice — interpreting God's 'sealing' of القلوب as a description of what humans have done to themselves rather than a direct divine act. The ثالثاً influence is the Mu'tazili position on divine attributes (al-sifat): they denied the independent reality of the divine attributes (العلم, power, will, etc.) and interpreted them as expressions of the divine essence, to avoid what they saw as a form of multiplicity in the divine being. This shapes how al-Zamakhshari handles the asma al-husna (divine names) and the attribute الآيات in the القرآن الكريم. Sunni العلماء — الأشعري, الماتريدي, and الأثري alike — identified and catalogued these theological interventions. Ibn al-Munir al-Iskandari (d. 683 AH), a المالكي scholar, wrote Al-Intisaf min Al-Kashaf (The Vindication Against Al-Kashaf), a comprehensive response to al-Zamakhshari's Mu'tazili glosses, refuting them from Sunni positions بينما quoting the Kashaf text verbatim. This work is often printed alongside Al-Kashaf in التقليدي editions, giving the reader both al-Zamakhshari's positions and the Sunni العلمي response on the same page. Later البالغ الأهمية readers include al-Taftazani, who addressed Al-Kashaf's theological positions in his commentary on theology, and numerous العلماء in the Ottoman and Mughal العلمي traditions who incorporated Al-Kashaf into their التفسير curricula بينما requiring students to study its theological passages alongsid
